title: Promote supported apps into a first-class native source picker
summary: Expose the existing supported-app catalog directly in the macOS source workflow so users can load known Humble repositories without detouring through the remote URL text field.
acceptance_criteria: The macOS toolbar offers a first-class supported-app picker that loads a known app directly.
The overview and source recovery flow offer supported apps when no native document is loaded or when recovery is needed.
The supported-app flow stays truthful about loading repository-backed remote exports rather than implying a local file import.
Ticket validation, patch hygiene, and a targeted HumbleStudioMac build pass for the slice.

evidence.note: HS-0009 ticket schema validated after adding the supported-app picker slice.
Patch hygiene passed for the supported-app picker changes.

evidence.path: xcodebuild -project HumbleStudio.xcodeproj -scheme HumbleStudioMac -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
evidence.note: Native macOS build succeeded after exposing supported apps in the toolbar, overview, and recovery workflow.

trigger.source_ref: Top 10 slice to offer supported repositories directly in the native source picker.

commands: xcodebuild -project HumbleStudio.xcodeproj -scheme HumbleStudioMac -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
agent_notes: This slice should reuse the existing supported app catalog instead of introducing a second source of truth.
The UX should clearly describe that supported apps still hydrate from repository-backed remote export URLs.
